title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Prompt Lens: Plan Mode
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt plan mode increment in a plan-execute-verify task contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Plan Mode
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Plan Mode contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.

content.text: Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Plan Mode, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a plan-execute-verify task contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Planning is overhead for every task regardless of complexity. This fails the Plan Mode contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Plan Mode evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-plan-mode-1ckqd0" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Plan Mode into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-plan-mode-1ckqd0
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Plan Mode failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Plan Mode, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Defend the Plan Mode model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Plan Mode applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
